steps.title: Identify the precise task
steps.content: The student wants to "specify how the salt enables energy storage." The notes spell out the mechanism: when molten salt solidifies at room temperature, energy stops flowing and is stored. A correct answer must show this cause-and-effect link — solidification causes energy to stop flowing, which is the storage mechanism — not just describe a property of the salt or an outcome of using the battery.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Scientists have developed a freeze-thaw battery that contains molten salt, which liquifies when heated and solidifies at room temperature.: This describes physical properties of molten salt but never explains that solidification causes energy to be stored. ✗
  • Choice B — The stored energy in a freeze-thaw battery, which contains molten salt, can be used by reheating the battery.: This describes how to release stored energy (thawing), not how storage occurs in the first place. ✗
  • Choice C — When the molten salt in a freeze-thaw battery solidifies at room temperature, energy stops flowing and can be stored in the battery.: This directly states the mechanism: solidification $\Rightarrow$ energy stops flowing $\Rightarrow$ energy is stored. That is exactly how the salt enables storage. ✓
  • Choice D — Molten salt allows a freeze-thaw battery to retain 92% of its charge after twelve weeks.: This states a performance statistic but gives no explanation of the storage mechanism. ✗
C is correct.
